title: Calhoun School
address: 433 West End Ave, New York, NY 10024, United States
open_hours: {"Friday":["8:30 am–4 pm"],"Monday":["8:30 am–4 pm"],"Saturday":["Closed"],"Sunday":["Closed"],"Thursday":["8:30 am–4 pm"],"Tuesday":["8:30 am–4 pm"],"Wednesday":["8:30 am–4 pm"]}
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Liz Murray","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWNzLEzDxhDuc0hWdDyWFRCq2OHNae6uJhDzw5935F7FHVfR_0PHA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I have a son in Kindergarden at Calhoun (where he also did the 4's), my daughter is starting there next year and I have been helping on a volunteer basis to teach creative writing in Calhoun Middle School for a few years now.

My husband and I did extensive research on several private schools in NYC before choosing Calhoun for our family. We couldn't be happier with this school. The class sizes are small. The kids get a very high amount of personal attention. The teachers are highly skilled, utilizing a progressive approach that meets the kids rightwhere they are in order to build the lesson out, driven by the child's curiosity and personal understanding.

We are a mixed race family and it was a great confront when we first started at Little Calhoun and saw that there are plenty of other mixed families.

Our son is very shy by nature and we were worried about him getting lost in the shuffle of a busy classroom because he's so quiet. He's also deeply sensitive and hyper aware of social rejection. My heart absolutely melted when he started coming home with stories of all the friends he's made and the experiments they do together in class. After observing the high quality teaching and extra personal attention given to each kid, I can say with confidence that our son is flourishing due to Calhoun's unusually supportive and challenging environment.

Now our son is coming home showing off some solid early reading skills, telling us stories about Martín Luther King and his ideas about what it takes to have a fair society. He is happy every day at school and he is absolutely thriving.

I couldn't recommend the school more highly. We are so grateful for Calhoun.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"David Elsberg","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIUkK8U4Bb_pJ63wqE4WUaIfeFkwu1K-Dx-8JwnltCK4a-xDg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Superb school.  The teachers know our children well and teach them in ways that reflect each one's individual style and interests.  The school creates an atmosphere in which the students love to learn.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Karigo Said","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLSpeQ2ro8eNO6sHYciy6h4dhZkMDduZn1MiuzgM4WdpjlT5Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Not bad.Beautiful compound.It's dope","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Tommy Denaro","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXHi7hlK9FJE1AIRh6vTdqGY11KMqt4j_jnXAaYmywsj1lCeima=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Amazing","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Dudie Silberman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIqd81h1KtaWfZllpfcY872IYIDLqnhhmA1UdAnW663Tdhzg1w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Top NYC school","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Lorenza Rodriguez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIv_pkaT9eYcaUBI6f3GgYj0sblPz7wZHQwA9tKoem-KA7wHg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Great school","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Australia Ramos","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXapJYxSt9RI5qdBrJwl4nHbk8EQM57nchsY8pf9MithNxHsf5U=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Jennifer Berry","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU8yj_mK38mD4UvQbke5nBz7aJzlj9VcWjfGqJajkMNAXNpWcz4tQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""}]

title: The Lang School
address: 26 Broadway Suite 900, New York, NY 10004, United States
open_hours: {"Friday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Monday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Saturday":["Closed"],"Sunday":["Closed"],"Thursday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Tuesday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Wednesday":["8 am–5 pm"]}
user_reviews: [{"Name":"Alan Cohen","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIZdQ8X-ihrliPkOhOp07u8muYM2qz1xqy0Ki6HPoQAj1Ot5Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"At Lang, our son feels that he finally found a place where he belongs and is learning that it's OK to be himself. He's building his identity, resilience, confidence and self-esteem. Lang offers intensive coordinated efforts to every individual student's well-being. They provide him with an individualized curriculum, resources and materials that satisfy our son's intellectual curiosities like no other school has ever done. For the first time since our son started his school life, he looks forward to going to school every morning. Lang also constantly communicates and collaborates with us as parents. It is not only a school but also a community for us, and this is a life-changing experience for our family. Having a kid with special needs is hard, and Lang has allowed us to connect with other parents who understand.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Joe Butler","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVfRDAhwSh9xjs8huwvjceClLWULEKbQTADqLDyK7Slo-AXOac=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I have had three kids attend The Lang School. My two oldest transitioned to a more traditional school for high school, and are doing amazingly well. I have zero doubt that their current success would not be happening absent all that Lang did for them. Lang is a wonderful, caring, nurturing place where 2e kids can learn at their level and acquire the skills they need to succeed in their future endeavors.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Lewis Fam","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWu698ANIUJp9yBWi3QPgheo9EV5umj56ukP3qpthCZpngxO8w=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This school is amazing- devoted and incredible teachers, wonderful support services and a collaborative and communicative environment. Our child has grown immensely at this school and we are so grateful to the whole staff!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Tatyana Gordon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKPKe0g1bEV2VB9KP3EpR9L7wrVi0DaPHH245SNlYCcNXYQCx7D=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"The children from this school steal hundreds of dollars of merchandise from the surrounding stores. 😒. What are they teaching kids these days. Shame.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Peter Flom","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUEPRJgRSNDGWXbGayM6riMDftDhwfK3D_2j4ceaeMIIgSYXJ9x=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Lang is a wonderful school serving a unique  and amazing group of students.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Elyssa Gaffin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIaZZ3zqzduvd6Tididc1K2d-e9mzrDbJ4FudS5FH7Qv27L5gc-=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"My son has spent 9 years as a Lang student. After a terrible experience at a mainstream private school, Lang offered a safe and nurturing environment where my son could thrive. We looked at countless special needs private schools, and there truly is no place like Lang. Their ability to challenge my son academically while giving him the scaffolding and support he needs has been incredible. My child loves Lang. He cannot wait to get there every day. The faculty and staff there are fully invested in my child's success and are great partners. If you have a 2E child, you will not find a better learning environment for your child.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Erin Heiser","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW90VbhoF7viimMzRmh1AxAkepwwEedlBucR_qy79Pr59zQLDqKQw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Every child needs teachers and adults in their life who can understand their challenges and their brilliance. The faculty and staff at Lang have this ability and the desire to help students grow into their own potential.  The school's vision of radical acceptance of each child's unique individuality makes Lang a truly wonderful place that has been life-changing for our child and for us as his parents.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Mary Miele","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVcKLa1E1Yd9SYWAz1CUsMj_8XSGhaOOIVzm8OfdfWNt4Hs0pHD3Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"As an education consultant I often work with the Lang School. It is an excellent choice for students who are gifted and who benefit from some learning support. Their teachers and therapists are wonderful, supportive and hold expertise in their positions! I highly recommend the Lang School to students and families in the NYC area!","Images":null,"When":""}]

title: Nord Anglia International School New York
address: 44 E 2nd St, New York, NY 10003, United States
open_hours: {"Friday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Monday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Saturday":["Closed"],"Sunday":["Closed"],"Thursday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Tuesday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Wednesday":["8 am–5 pm"]}
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Emma Neath","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJAX3FfMUOsCY3tNXMQiDNLHEeCc0vlS9ga0vwxh6iQsnwLRg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This school is incredible. I can't speak highly enough of the teachers, administrators, facilities team, students and parents that are involved with the school. Due to the highly passionate teachers, my children have developed into confident and conscientious students. With the small class sizes each child receives individualised attention, developing skills like resilience, grit, and a growth mindset. The students are all well mannered and caring of each other and their community, wanting to participate in all activities from sports, the school production, community outreach and UN activities. The families that attend are from NY and the rest of the world, making up a wonderfully diverse community that is welcoming and kind. This is the best school in NYC!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Maria Dahlquist-Collins","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUpBX__5ZiSBDVBEg3Sxkw3LqAOwWisJtdrLH8_nAe5tkVvrCa5=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This school is a hidden gem amongst the concrete jungle of Manhattan. You feel welcome the minute you walk into the building. The staff is all very friendly, helpful, open & available, and takes the time to get to know you and your child; Even the teachers from different grades. It's a very warm and welcoming community.

My daughter has been here for 3 years now, having moved from our local public school after feeling overwhelmed by the class sizes. We were attracted to Nord Anglia because of the combination of Inquiry Based Learning, small class sizes with individualized curriculums, the artists in residence program, and the MIT collaborations. With a daughter who is very interested in both art and science, this school is the perfect fit. Lessons are constructed in a way that carries a theme throughout different subject matters, giving the children an opportunity to look at it from multiple disciplines, which makes it very easy for the children to digest and retain the information. I'm always impressed with the passion my daughter brings home

It really is an amazing school. I only wish it went all of the way through High School!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Craig H","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKbwZY6Y__7ipw3m5ApJGaHtN7kZpYFxAzv5oTbQRyuXnVmxw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We welcome visitors and prospective parents to our school at anytime! Every few days we have interactive online meetings and with the small class size, it is easy to cover tons of information in an extremely interactive fashion.
Dwight global definately offers some of the toughest content you can find online and are very adaptive to your needs, allowing you to take higher level classes if you desire. I love Dwight Global and would recommend it to anyone who is looking to pursue a good schooling as well as their passions and dreams.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Adriana Moura","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVbm1M6UqD5LxgvxqVdHdRI6y0FWe8mG3EakVPWoRa-kvAUscfHNw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We moved from Brazil 10 months ago and, simply put, our experience at Dwight has been sensational! The philosophy of nurturing each student's spark of genius is real and we could never imagine our sons having such unique, unforgettable experiences (like performing at Carnegie Hall and taking part in an off Broadway musical) in such a short period of time. She is now in third grade and loves this school. I have never met more of an amazing community. I have been actively involved with the school from the start. Their constant strive for innovation, academics and achieving is what has impressed me the most.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Pasha Solonin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ4yiuQagOKAGgwpUZbWCY02FNTyxs6ZLpzxTp6zk1LlHToEQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Hello. I am Pasha Solonin. I have been to four different schools so far and I must say that Dwight is a really good school for a few reasons. I spent a full academic year at Dwight ( 2015-2016)and I enjoyed being in the school. Back then I was in seventh grade.  Dwight offers great education in subjects like English, Social Studies, Math and Science. I remember that in English I’ve read novels such as The Tempest, Lord of the Flies, and The Outsiders. I learned about symbolism in the novels I’ve read.  Dwight was a very welcoming school. This school has a great counselor who helped me solve the problems whenever I encountered them.

There is only one concern I have for Dwight and that is the use of their QUEST program.In early November, my parents received an email where the QUEST program was offered to help me in areas where I needed help. My parents never gave a proper answer of yes or no. A few days later I was forced to go into the QUEST program against my own will and the will of my parents.   QUEST program attempted to offer me support where I simply didn’t need it. That is the only reason why I hated the program. My parents later on revealed to me the price of the program which was as expensive as a year spent at Dwight. As days passed by the QUEST program was removed from my schedule, but it remained in school since then. I got worried about other students who might also fall into this trap.

The QUEST program was used like a loot box in EAs (Electronic Arts) most disappointing video game, Star Wars Battlefront II. Another words the QUEST program was used in a greedy way.

I am hoping that one day this program will either be removed, or becomes beneficial to students and helps them whenever they need help in understanding certain topics.

I hope this explains all my thoughts on Dwight. I still think that it is a good school when it comes to its  atmosphere and its fantastic education.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Prisha Vyas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXqjApKIkUzLwcgI_X8ZDNsyiEfWJ9arq2HuLmX6rp9YPd1BzYNPw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Dwight Global really helped me find a balance between school and tennis training in my busy tournament and practice schedule! Thanks to Dwight I can take all of the classes that I want and be able to graduate high school, while also trying to go pro in tennis.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Mabel Moreno","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVwhKgjjNZNuE9C_SyV6f3FjKTKfKkTZOza7amkfKT0ssri6lksMA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Really good going there currently but will be moving soon back to Australia Dwight school if you are looking I will miss you!!","Images":null,"When":""}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Angela Ortiz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIRU7N2yAs7DOHbqTqNXjtQUQQpRTAv5iO3GzHlfOEzgk-7cg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"My daughter heard about CAS (City As School) from a friend who had a similar academic experience and told her that she'd never been happier in school.  I was skeptical to say the least when my daughter asked to attend an Open House at the start of school last year.  I wish that more parents and students knew that CAS existed as an excellent option for students who are struggling in mainstream schools which is why I am offering this review.

My daughter has attended selective NYC schools since elementary school, but because of her ADHD, her academic experience has been a roller coaster.  She was accepted into a very selective HS with a 5% acceptance rate.  Her first two years were very frustrating and disheartening because academically the school was a wrong fit for her due to her issues with Executive Functioning.  At home we provided a lot of support and she worked round the clock to maintain a B average because as an academically rigorous school there was a huge work load.  Although she was keeping up, her confidence dwindled because her efforts were not translating into the grades she wanted.  She also wondered how she would keep up going away to college without all the support.

Needless to say it was extremely risky to decide to transfer schools, especially  in her Junior year.  She had started the year on strong footing which made it an even harder decision, but she saw it as leaving on a "good note".   Academically she had already learned a lot, but needed to learn more life skills in order help her succeed in college.  I knew of so many cases where the parents were doing so much for their kids at home that when they went off to college they could not do it on their own.  This was a frightening thought and was definitely part of our decision.

As a parent, the first few months were nerve-wracking  because the CAS model is so different than that of her prior school.  The students time is spent 50/50 doing internships and classes.  The internships offer academic credits and an array of experiences from working with a Judge to working in many   different businesses.  The students are in charge of selecting their internships and classes during the registration process which occurs every 8 weeks.  A lot of the structure of the school is designed to foster independence and confidence.  The teacher's help students understand how to interact with adults in the real world.

I was part of a parent group for a School Quality Review last month for which they got the highest possible rating.  We shared similar experiences with kids from high achieving schools who felt overwhelmed or out of place.  The reviewer asked us why we chose CAS and with each story there were tears.  The tears were for all the years of frustration not knowing how to help our kids. They were  also of happiness that our kids finally found a school where they could regain confidence and mature.  While teachers at CAS have great empathy, they are tough and have high expectations.  The Principal gives out his cell number and everyone I've experienced at the school makes time to communicate with students and parents whether on the phone or by email even on the weekends.

My daughter is excelling in school, taking college classes, and studying for SAT's.  She is excited to come home and share her experiences which she rarely did before.  She has not missed a single day of school or internship and is excited to share her experience with incoming students who are admitted on a rolling basis.

So far the staff has been exceptionally caring, smart, and wiling to go out of their way.  Of course no place is perfect.  The students are definitely a "mixed bag", but the ones who want to do well and have parental support can truly thrive.  The ones who don't have this have the support of the amazing staff.

The college office offers incredible support and resources which I have not seen in many NYC Public HS's.  At CAS students graduate with a plan for their future whether it's college or work, they are taught to deal in the real world.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Rocio Ramirez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU1GuGMv-khlEDos_KiSDVLk9wjqxsf_m05KAtDT2JMjCBWFDL5=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"AWESOME SCHOOL !!!!The graduation rate is high so thats a good thing to keep in mind.  When I was unsure if I was going to ever make it out of high School a good friend of mine suggested city as & it was the perfect school for me. The teachers help you so much they care, Rachel, Vicent , Veronica, Marcus, Andrew, Rita, Joseline, Gary, art teacher, Melissa, tomiko, omg I can say all of the teachers ! they help me so much. I really suggest this school if you want to meet great people and if you want to graduate, they have tutoring, Saturdays schools, esl, ,. The key is to stay focused and do all your work and assignments and you will pass. You'll feel like home there, the internships are awesome , its in the city beautiful, clean classrooms, the halls are full of art and you meet a lot of great souls. The teachers help you always I never been to a school so dedicated to their students.from the security guards they super cool .  I graduated and I still miss it there with all these great humans more like family. Thank you for everything you guys are a blessing !","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Eddie O.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUNWS5snj8zgvQvo-TovKRYGakrEC8DU7AYJfS3wDXp2Xpgwl2cEQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I graduated from city-as-school in June of 1995, yes, it does help you out to graduate and an eye opener especially if you were struggling thru high school and wasn't able to finish on time. I needed an extra year of school and attended city-as-school for one year and I was able to graduate and receive my hs diploma. This school is perfect for kids who are struggling to graduate to open their eyes and also a second chance in hs life to get it right. Trust the teachers and they will give you confidence in graduating on time.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Hasan Brown","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV5yxQ6ALl_RS2Dmqe57XAqAhjcRP3OnTJCBo6J8KzCz-NMLz3hcQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Best public school in the city. Great teachers that sacrifice a lot. Great experience for students. Great environment for all. Love this school, I am a graduate... Make good choices. - Holly S.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Chloe Blackshire","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVx8P-ZyS5SJVIm6labzy-Tmf7UD0rmJYrnJHEoSOweOOsKFtDP=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Absolutely loved this school. City As opened up so many opportunities for me","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Peter Gonzalez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLaf76NW3q7F-ZgBfe2RsVM-6XgwZIMTAZI9O36x0I5eC9WXg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Was never able to be in a class until i came to this school.  Was the greatest years of my life.
Finish school in the year 2000, greatest instructors and teachers I have ever had.
For all walks of life and more.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Ed P.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUJc12WGXxqxWBGwJdtq-JuW0ZlIUVIimwCBGmhp8TSsXxvRtNf=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Graduated in '04. If you are attending City-As, it means you have probably struggled at some point during your high school career. I was no different. Without City-As, I probably wouldn't have graduated. I learned valuable life skills, and had some dedicated teachers. I am very grateful to have found this school. I graduated instead of dropping out or getting a GED like so many of my peers at other schools, went to a community college, eventually wound up at a four year school, and today I have a graduate degree. City-As is not perfect and still deals with many of the problems that public schools face, but I owe this school a lot.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"don brassco","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWDUpCS50Dad8F3dKj3Tq6Jd4xRp1rdoxU4yKFUdS3Vfra4aJhn=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"my god bro was the first class MEL WILLIAMS....LOVE THE SCHOOL....","Images":null,"When":""}]

title: Notre Dame School
address: 327 W 13th St, New York, NY 10014, United States
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Kris Benjamin Randolph","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVEPWBdU_oew6knIgLTjisuEIVqB0PGzKhJ7-3JYl02_4S--bFQWQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I graduated from Notre Dame in the mid 90's so I cannot speak with any relevance to the schools current academic curriculum or activities. But, I can say this---I have done well and it is due in large part to the foundation that Notre Dame provided.

I am conversant on a range of topics and can  fit  in almost every level of society and a broad range of academic circles. I am knowledgeable about physics, art, literature, business and so much more. The small classroom meant I could not hide. I could not be invisible and despite my best efforts to only like some classes and ignore others, I am even pretty decent in math.
This became handy when I later decided I  wanted to go to business school.

People often assumed with my sharp wit and erudite manner--I must have been top of my class. And that is far from true. My purpose of Notre Dame was to serve the balance that made the top half possible.

I was determine to be free thinking and somewhat raw --in my intelligence. But the practice of being discipline was a healthy preparation for first the academic rigor of college and later the world of politics, that allows for very few mistakes. Notre Dame is not perfect by any stretch of the imagination. But it is worth its value in tuition. And should you be lucky to be accepted it is a tremendous opportunity to create in you a foundation that will bring great things.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kevin Barrett","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXVM0HMWJb0MO6ssukYTxA7iP3fiSn_cdsFdDV4NHlwqI1wAVqy=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Impressive...Impressive. open_hours: {"Friday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Monday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Saturday":["Closed"],"Sunday":["Closed"],"Thursday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Tuesday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Wednesday":["8 am–5 pm"]}
user_reviews: [{"Name":"Lilliana Aponte","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK2IpdKUD87EzRbx5aMkxnuDZ_QibDBlnkEFyYFnSq_TBWCsg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"My child started at Maryel School of New York at the age of 2 years old and for the past 8 years, the school has provided an environment full of kindness, compassion, caring and a kind of nurturing that makes the school so special. My child loves Maryel School and has consistently scored in the 99th percentile in NYS DOE standardized exams, which is a testament to their academic excellence as well and their dedication to students’ education. The teachers and directors, Juan and Celine Cacho go out of their way to ensure that students and families are heard and their commitment to the students’ well being is evident in all the little happy faces we see throughout the school. We feel very fortunate to have found Maryel School at an early age, as our child is now fully bilingual and very well prepared in both languages, English and Spanish, to start middle school, as all preschool through elementary school subjects are taught in both languages, at an organic pace that makes learning a second language fun and engaging. With much gratitude to the teachers and administrators, I highly recommend Maryel School of New York!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipPaf2QMsf8WlHh7Pre1_wkCi6UirItkOWpnxSPg\u0026fid=0x0:0xf9773ad7b8b9a974"],"When":"2024-10-24"},{"Name":"Melissa Hernandez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXlBdixjNlo9WeiNiSxzNk8ydnNoN_KiYq_M8HmcO5LGrA2DVQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Our twin boys have been at Maryel since preschool. They are now in second grade. We could not ask for a better school. We love being able to give them the gift of being Bilingual. They are learning all subjects in both languages, which is a priceless education. I believe the teachers to be the best in NYC and the administrative staff are outstanding. Our boys are happy, eager to get to school daily, and safe. As a working mother, I am worry free throughout my day, knowing the caring, capable and compassionate hands they are in. Maryel deserves more than five stars!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Carmen Matos","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWKs7fqwH7AIH7l5Ht_nRQePljbQVHRwBbb3jTaXHPXg1Vtea4=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"My granddaughters attend this school and they are learning a lot of interesting subjects including Spanish. address: 62 Avenue B, New York, NY 10009, United States
open_hours: {"Friday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Monday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Saturday":["Closed"],"Sunday":["Closed"],"Thursday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Tuesday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Wednesday":["8 am–5 pm"]}
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Tamami T","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJIog-nMQe29438N_G-zmdzFk5s-XrJ33O2oRUMhTZmQC_97A=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"New Amsterdam School has a lots of healthy and nourishment of childhood experience. Children will be spending a lot of outside experience,They will have so many discovery of beauty in the nature in the busy city everyday. The daily Meal for early childhood is offered by school made by Organic non gmo meal.They get to hear stories and songs along with seasonal cerebration of nature and culture everyday, play time with full of imagination with beautiful natural toys.Beautiful arts,crafts as a weekly routine, a lot of love,warmth and attentive care to individual children from teachers,and warm community members and friends are opening arms. Children loves to go school everyday, and have love of learning experience. Highly recommended!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Robert Gilson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXgeL6Mngrm9qefhdjs0ZMnqVQkGL5Cz1kquqUnhcbQhAy7rhk=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The New Amsterdam School is one of the most extraordinary educational opportunities in New York City. Inspired by the Waldorf educational pedagogy, New Am honors the natural, intuitive learning process of children, a process that occurs in its time, un-rushed by political agenda or institutionalized educational flavors of the month. Children discover learning through stories, imagination, exploration, and play; not drills, worksheets, or test prep. The classrooms are enchanting places, the faculty delightful and caring, and the children experience nature every day of the year, rain or shine, hot or cold, in a community garden a short walk away.

As a parent of a New Amsterdam child I treasure the insights they have cultivated in me about how to be the best parent for our modern times. Our children create their play, they don't purchase it. They create their entertainment, they don't just consume it. And they expand their vocabulary and imagination with stories and songs that are steeped in tradition and wisdom, not in marketing and merchandising.

It is no wonder that Waldorf schools score highest in intrinsic motivation reported by students than any other educational model; they learn because they enjoy learning. I teach middle school math in the mainstream public system, and I bristle at the external, and often arbitrary, pressures imposed upon my students. What Waldorf schools do is so unlike what I have been taught to do in my classroom. They have exceptional curriculum and an inspiring long-view of the child as a learner and citizen, something institutionalized testing does its best to extinguish. No Child Left Behind, Common Core, all the initiatives public schools roll out every few years feel like groping in the dark compared to the sophistication yet simplicity of the Waldorf model.

I have researched its pedagogy and have shared my discoveries with my colleagues and my students. Both have truly enjoyed the nuggets we've explored. In fact, my middle school students count our Waldorf exploration into geometric constructions as one of their favorite units of the year. Their work is gorgeous.

I encourage all parents to explore its offerings, take on the challenge, and experience life with the originality and ingenuity we deserve.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kaarin Von","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUXQWmhiyA3gDbeC__xDcA9b-uOvyuexvgxw_9hMB8fPaOOmfs=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This school has been like a second home for my child. She has grown and thrived academically, emotionally, socially and in a true inward way. New Amsterdam has been a blessing for our family in so many ways. We couldn't be happier with the school and its approach to education.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Isaac Dodd","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJIuDm9UtIUzLEd-Be2qR0Utkk5oyn1N_l_0KXustfeR0xdI4s=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"New Amsterdam School was good but the place was not good.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Eve Teresa","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIoVJ2k9Mdr--ZXpkq2Pq83VdWh4pPi7hZFqlmMowzfPglKUA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"My children love going to this school. The outdoor program, the community, and the teachers make this place exceptional. My daughter spends the whole morning outside in the garden and I feel a little less guilty having her grow up in Manhattan!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Jennie Montgomery","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIhjTf6-m90LIsMC9Op53e-7sfSoCtcYYOIn6TNzUYcThbLVQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We LOVE our school.  It's the only Waldorf school in downtown Manhattan and we are so lucky to have found it.  Beautiful peaceful surroundings, devoted teachers, an inspired philosophy, time in the garden every day, natural whole foods, wonderful handcrafts, art, music etc. My children have been there since babies through Parent Child Classes, Nursery, and Kindergarten and now there's the Grades School too.  A lovely intimate school and great community for the parents too.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Guta Hedewig","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUpUgDe3DZRB1ViYzfLAe8jwP5oyTOXRl_h_F5v_N8l0qVOx24Y=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"New Amsterdam School is an extraordinary experience for our two children! We've been attending classes there for four years now, and the beautiful classrooms, loving teachers, inspired philosophy underlying the teachings, all make for a well-rounded foundation for young children that can't be found anywhere else in lower Manhattan. It's definitely worth trying one of the Open House sessions to get a sense of the incredible care and thoughtfulness of the school's approach.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Neil Baptista","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVF9YzsGJmrZK1Nd1XjqvlaFzuEgAbu4u87g5VSHQwkRCOcDSo=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The teachers are caring, thoughtful and playful. Kids get plenty of fresh air outdoors in a huge community garden, healthy snacks and lots of love. It feels like a wonderful country school in the middle of the East Village. Children come out of this place happy, bright and curious.","Images":null,"When":""}]

title: The Beekman School
address: 220 E 50th St, New York, NY 10022, United States
open_hours: {"Friday":["8:30 am–5 pm"],"Monday":["8:30 am–5 pm"],"Saturday":["Closed"],"Sunday":["Closed"],"Thursday":["8:30 am–5 pm"],"Tuesday":["8:30 am–5 pm"],"Wednesday":["8:30 am–5 pm"]}
user_reviews: [{"Name":"Judith “Judy” Reinicke","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU-afHNbFYcytWm9riSt5INlHLG0s-TOFHihlAL8yBLSGBUM_o=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I first discovered The Beekman School in 2005 when my older son had left a boarding school that was not a good fit, and we found ourselves looking for a school for his senior year, not an easy task. My husband had died unexpectedly the year before, so I was doing this on my own. Arriving at our appointment, Headmaster George Higgins immediately made us feel comfortable, and was most encouraging. Under his direction the application process was easy, and the transition smooth. My son soon found himself with wonderful teachers who were eager to bring out his strengths, and as his confidence grew, he quickly began earning the best grades of his life. The very small class sizes were ideal in helping him to focus, and if extra support was needed, The  Tutoring School within Beekman was right there to help. He also quickly made friends, finding the other Beekman students lively and diverse, friendly and caring. He still keeps in touch with many of them. He had an amazing senior year, and only wished he had been able to benefit from all four years at  Beekman.\nIn 2012, my younger son, adopted from Eastern Europe, was experiencing some reading comprehension challenges, and although passing, was struggling at his school. There was also some teasing going on. I called George Higgins again, and he welcomed us warmly, as returning family members. My younger son started Beekman in his sophomore year, and to our delight, many of the teachers are still there, all these years later. Having always been a good Math student, my son quickly started to receive top grades in Algebra and Physics,  and  for the first time did well in English and History, challenging subjects for him before. The individual attention Beekman offers has made him blossom, and he has been an Honor Roll student.  He has made many friends, and there is no teasing. He is part of Student Government, and loves going to school.  He  will graduate in 2015. I couldn't be more grateful to George Higgins and the dedicated teachers of  The Beekman School, for giving  both my sons such an excellent and enjoyable high school experience.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Milagros Soriano","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWA6IXGOBbFwWt7FX4OdlDPjJZocKEkPVtx3iob0foE_nBkO60=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Is small school easy to aaccess for my daughter","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Susan Reich","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVLb2aUxT0pAAZ1hfLJ4zXXtFCNHkm-u6YJwE2MAMtSE4inWG4=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"This school is really tiny, just 70 or so kids across four grades. This has its advantages: small class sizes, family feel, nobody gets lost. When you call, the principal picks up the phone. You feel like you're not just a number. That size has its disadvantages: no sports teams, not a lot of fancy facilities. It's very DIY: if you find an arts program you like at SVA or a drama or dance course somewhere else in the city, the school will count that course as a credit, if it's long/rigorous enough, so you can really take advantage of NYC. IF you are in a sports league, the school will credit that as a sport. There's a (pretty reasonably priced) one-on-one option if the kid is really behind on something that you can take instead of the Beekman class. On the downside, there isn't a wide range of art or music or P.E. offered within the school. (They go to a local gym on Fridays) Beekman seems to have a lot of more or less normal kids who for whatever reason aren't a classic fit for a typical school. Some of them are expats who came halfway through the semester, or from the U.N, (there's quite a lot of Asian kids), some are from fancy private or big public schools where they stumbled. I guess Beekman is their second chance, and some of them take it and others don't and get moved along. This is not a competitive school to get into, but there are some quite talented kids here. The teachers seem kind, if not universally dazzling pedagogues. There's no PTA (that I've found) and no fundraising. MY LD kid is there, after a few stumbles. Overall we're pleased with how sane and flexible the school is; low key, sensible, no crazy NYC pressure, while still getting kids a solid education. I would recommend Beekman for the kid who needs something a bit different, but is still recognizably a school.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Mohan Kumar Khangar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUQE8LIo5FRs9b--WxToIdZIjYUS28lY9QHZ5fGRyq6kYU8fqkUsw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Are you curious come here\n...","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipPgWezncCYUhsKTU4gDpL_hi-s6y_2ZIMEq534v\u0026fid=0x0:0x5cf693001d7f8e22"],"When":"2017-3-7"},{"Name":"Isabella P","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWYKf-nj7w9UegZO_pG_xWbMfabbch8UIS1vzox0LKZgxspn85BpQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Emma Ramos","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXqZx5XCBzq3nqM7azMdCxOkNWhNQUPr36_EnXe1eH4vhqZlws4=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"张潍","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJPa2Fkyv2sJfwaOI337Qu_uVxwr9ynzziL7xyQ5rd0tJWqGg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Samia Brahimi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIGpSMEekNdBdTyLrwYZSTupv3iNYQU7NI2QXHUrXX8OxpnRQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""}]

title: Loyola School

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"C W","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJJwvLC_jWXZ1wecbgS58q5-6NGQt9SdFMmTxGBj3i8svsSMPra=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Our daughter applied to this school for kindergarten many years ago. They claimed to be a diverse environment but they were not. We were too naive to trust them and paid for the admission fees. When we toured the school, we were surprised to see their graduate classes to be prevailing white students. No jokes. Of course our daughter who is physically identified as Asian got rejected. They said they were selective. BUT, our daughter later took the NYC gifted & talented exam and scored 99%. She got into the gifted program and we were proud of her.

According to the data on polarislist.com, the  Ivy school acceptance rate at Loyola High School is 3.77% (2 out of 53 graduates) from 2018 to 2020. Sorry, this data didn’t prove what Loyola claimed themselves to be.

This review is to warn the other prospect applicants to Loyola who are also people of color. To apply or not is up to your own discretion, but we don’t recommend this school according to our terrible experience.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Jeff Valenzuela","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUbhVivXOQczrOPtgp6Wa_dTZlRVVfgqtojUWWGZR68IbMDKF7S=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Had a great experience during my time as a student at this wonderful place. I highly recommend for anyone entering high school who is looking for a Jesuit and well rounded secondary education!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Carly Ortiz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVJ6gqE4_CyxPo7iyWH9IFsjlzmr-hyxbhjoejPOU_qlxMR_3U=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"You rejected a student who is determined, dedicated, and an excellent student. Also if you don't offer scholarships why you telling people you do. Anyway screw this school and the relative I'm talking about will be successful without you or your school. [{"Name":"Inna Swenson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIg_yuDuQXf5cYZQQ83GxNMbndf8szazeKGX5o6Vzp_t2vdFA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"My two daughters attend the school, currently in first grade and junior kindergarten. The education is unparalleled in every aspect - mathematics, language, performing arts, art, music, and technology. The teachers are committed, enthusiastic, and dedicated and it shows everyday in the classroom in everything that they do. The programming is well planned and inspired, from school musicals and performances and weekly children-led assemblies to the sports curriculum (basketball, swimming, soccer) and ongoing field trips around New York City! Spanish immersion has been incredible with my daughters starting to speak and read in the language and really enjoy it. The head of school has founded and led schools all over the world, has a deep understanding of education and a has a wonderful hands-on approach.

My older daughter is somewhat shy and soft-spoken in public, but at Pine Street School she is driven to be a risk-taker and has been presenting at weekly assemblies in front of large parent audiences, with her voice getting louder and more confident with each passing week.

The school is focused on developing students equipped to succeed not only today but in the future in our ever changing society.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Geoff Bailey","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX6FzW0tz05y9lpTJnwzwqVK8xLiZ2kLPImN_2Bstk4Er9sHTvUaw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"My son and daughter attend Pine Street and I cannot say enough about the school. title: Regis High School
address: 55 E 84th St, New York, NY 10028, United States
open_hours: {"Friday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Monday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Saturday":["Closed"],"Sunday":["Closed"],"Thursday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Tuesday":["8 am–5 pm"],"Wednesday":["8 am–5 pm"]}
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"George Corrent","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKN_YR0WhFClLKuzfrcCdv-D3rkKoPQVknT9JnVB-Z1R3OUog=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One of the best educational experiences I ever had - I proceeded to go to Duke, Phd at Rice University, MD, Residency at Washington Univ. St Louis, U. of M.-Bascom Palmer Eye Institute, Cleveland Clinic, etc.
Regis prepared me to learn. There are worse things one can get out of 4 years of HS.
I never saw cheating, but I guess it goes on some everywhere (haven't there been a few cheating "scandals" at the military academies?); but I doubt there was much; our teachers really paid attention to our work, they knew what we were capable of; it would have been difficult to suddenly ACE a Final after getting 65-75% on the rest of the grades and classwork.
My wife went to a very highly ranked HS in the burbs of NJ; even she admits the education I got in HS was far beyond what her school offered; And unlike the view of the writer who panned the school and students, I wasn't a great studier; played basketball (until my grades weren't good enough one semester- then I was off), and didn't graduate very high in my class ranking; but I was prepared for everything that followed.
So, I'd rank it pretty highly.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Oskar Schmidt","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJh8BkCjUR4cPhA8dor3fS-d8RfW0d2sASCZFhfdD-_KT66=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"In January 1956 I was awarded a 72 in Latin and, because I had gotten under 75 in my previous semester, I was dropped from Regis. I never did any more than the absolute minimum necessary in all my subjects and Latin tripped me up. I deeply regret not having done more. Went on to Don Bosco Prep in Ramsey, NJ and got a 90 average im my last year and a half, doing no more than at Regis. I never learned to study because I never needed to, from grammar school on. Got my BA from Maryland after a year and a half at Holy Cross and a M.Ed. from Boston University. Often wonder what my life would be like if I had learned to work and study hard. Everything came too easy without doing anything.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kelvyn Palacios Perez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU5jFf1z_1YsEq02iT3q48I3EU5LsXlGrAj-YJ30O5OGsaKvAzz=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It is a very good high school. Maybe the best and it help us as better students.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Bob Skiba","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVO3PagL7NJoWyfZeDmP6BJ3wllb9HsYezVY5hbdsYQUHXMIhI=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Regis made a dramatic difference in my life. It not only taught me how to learn, it taught me how to think.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"tom ryan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLcNPt5fD2gteZW66FXyH8bgqTcvPTkX5-3NaqY2BXpoA4h=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I spent four challenging years at Regis and developed learning and action methods that have served me eell. The faculty is caring and the atmosphere is collegial. It is a great school.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"OldCorpsEd","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIPwAMIj4GNhTKTk-JSPJuVejUhLQWkJfUFFD7vgHJ-MuSfWg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The best years of my life along with my years as a Marine Corps officer and pilot. At Regis I learned to think, to question, to challenge, to learn and to seek more knowledge. In the Corps I learned to lead, and to conquer fear. Together, the experiences have given me a fulfilling life through its ups and downs. May ours be the noble heart.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Chris Demeke","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXuKij-a4L9JcFsXj6gkjqHRNpy_obXrqyju2bKSIWujRK5LGcfCg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Pretty good Basketball courts. Part of NYC basketball league's. Downstairs basketball courts is very narrow tho, but good for three on three.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Brendon Alvarez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKRsiLc68aTPBDx7R9v1nOp_COMCIsWAXXKE7aLFv3oJRgNkg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Best catholic school in all of NY, amazing experiences and a very friendly environment.","Images":null,"When":""}]

Great school too work at","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"allan zhang","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV9Ha_sBUMptLIvl3H6_YV1WTKOuDeF4ywlbM5uroOLl7HkSk5S=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"I played here for ABCHOOPSNYC.  The gym is wood but not polished/waxed (at least not when I played there).  The lighting is dim aka not bright enough.  The bathroom was ok. It did not smell. There is a tiny gym behind the main gym but the ceiling is quite low. The lines painted seem to be out of sync. Overall I do not enjoy playing here.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Jamie Goldstein","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKusRA_aMXMPNxZzRJg0W4dH4UbdUR4UKsf3k8JYoXP01nAdg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"my old school had 3 gyms... 2 on 5 and one on 6","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"5 Subscriber's With No Video's Challenge","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWju7vwJjohuQvMtCiu6XQpE9-jHEJja1GiUKxiLbwU3o1U1yM=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"When I went to Murry Bergtraum in the 1980's  it was a specialized school for students who had passed their previous school with no lower than a 85% average and the policy at this school was that you had to maintain an 80 average in order to remain in the school. The school also had a strict conduct policy. You must respect teachers and fellow classmates, insubordination resulted in a transfer or dismissal after 3rd warning. The school was located right across from One Police Plaza. what better security. The school was supported by AT\u0026T, the building it was attached to and it was not far from the heart of Wall Street where I worked after school three days a week at a brokerage firm. Because of the elite computer program major, where I interned at 7am in the morning doing \"keypunch\" , IBM would recruit young programmers right from the school. One of my classmates took introduction to law and went to John Jay college at night. One girl dressed in a three piece suit everyday because she managed a department at one of the local companies. When you left this school you left with a career. I loved and was extremely proud of this school when I left it in 1984'. Bring it back to it's former glory, which means the standards of a magnet school part charter. \"Only those meeting the criteria with teacher recommendation should enter..\". They have their district schools they can attend that are standard with special needs programs with children that have behavorial situations (that is important) Also, bump them if they don't want to respect , NO MATTER THEIR ACADEMIC ACHIEVEMENT\" RESPECT IS KEY\" ..Look for new and inventive sponsors who will pour into this school like the giants of the past. Reach out to former alumni , such as one of the \"Wayne brothers\" to see what they are willing to do. Then revamp this school back to its former glory. Many kids were like me, I traveled almost 1hr to get to school everyday from Harlem to be in a school where a bully was NOT ALLOWED. There were majors like accounting, law, computer science and programming, typing, and many others. There was a girl there who had one good hand in a wheel chair who typed 90 words a minute , put me to shame. I took computer science and accounting and had 84 credits when I graduated. Now when the world will have to think out of the box to bring itself back from the dead this school can be like a phoenix rising from the ashes. Bill Gates, Oprah Winfrey, This is a school worth fighting for....Please help Murry Bergtraum  Shine AGAIN!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"NYC","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWnBIpN8aAqn0nNzKftp0hfxuvK-UO2K4SqjDlERyAu684A35Ox=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Murry Bergtraum High School is located in the Lower East Side section of Manhattan, across from One Police Plaza and James Madison Plaza and walking distance from the Brooklyn Bridge and City Hall.\n\nBergtraum, German origin, in English translates to \"mountain dream.\" The United Nations flag stands as a sign of Peace.\"New York City Department of Education.\"","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kazz Hardy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI2iGcEi19Oa-0Pofgu65GnmWHZE6EY3KmwfqTMUTQvjBJ5ybo=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Teachers were very caring honest and nice... students were nice and cool to each other through out my years there very great school... help me get where I'm at in life now... despite how awful I was when I was there","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kym Watson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVPYlC6JjbJGK3ezD0ThXTOwGXtzTpdhVdn3UQjAIqCzUu1gHqMLA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"One of my children was a student there for three years from 2014-2017. She was a student recovering from tragic life events who also struggled academically and socially. The team at Murry Bergtraum, headed by Principal Naima Cook, was terrific. They were patient, supportive and helped her with on-site resources. They did this while keeping their \"eyes on the prize\" and the focus on moving steadily toward the goal of graduation. They convened regular meetings that were replete with \"straight talk\" about my child's performance and trajectory on the path to completing high school. I am grateful for their support. Along with Ms. Cook, big shout-outs to Ms. David, Mr. Peguero and Ms. Emmanuel, whose patience and dedication make them candidates for sainthood!😊","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"wei ni","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXq-5yJgCpM06ZPJvws4JHt23MQpOp-pcvwa6hnlxYizVVWJciL=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Many different schools in one building. EMS in the basement, MB on ground floor and 2nd floor, MECA on 3rd floor, UAM on 4th floor.","Images":null,"When":""}]

user_reviews: [{"Name":"Chelsea Frazer","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU_4CKXrscqWohkCfOTexlEC2yf1izA-sD8xOlKiMqZD7P89qNI=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"My time at the gateway school was more or less one of the worst experiences in my life. I loved all the teachers and friends I met there  however, the administrators did not know how to handle people with emotional issues. (I’m bipolar) and so they always made us feel like we were doing something wrong. Not only did they tell all the high schools I applied to that I was emotionally unstable (which is absolutely not true)  but because of them I was forced to attend a high school where all the kids were “recovering drug addicts”.\n\nIf you have a child with emotional issues then this school is NOT for you.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Ben Jagger","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVZTVInf_DapyeeKH1JNz9Wq_YTZjuKZ10M7VX1X12vfeNqFVI=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"I went here, I didn't like it, but they did teach me how to read so 2 stars instead of 1","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Ian Fury","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK7frBfJgmUkyYOfbG_8KcPuZk_TNj3NN11yIdymiVn-6McaA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wonderful school with dedicated teachers. It’s a academically rich place with very kind-hearted families.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Tommy Susman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLamTuReOiD6y_DZCEgq--nsbYlTrjw831y_C_AWxv33pyrnA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Went here from 5-8 grade it was alright. the teachers were alright but more than half of the teachers had left the school by the time i did. Furthermore I do feel that the curriculum does not prepare kids for general education i was the only kid in my grade to go to a public high school. We were taught at a very slow pace and the teachers did not know how to get the kids to behave so a lot of days were wasted. Also the lower grades were full of entitled rich kids who would throw a tantrum  whenever they did not get what they wanted. The principle had many events to try have some fun but those were futile attempts at trying to get the kids to like going to school at gateway (none of the kids liked it). Additionally the teachers are paid almost nothing for a private school in NYC and that's with the school asking for donations from all the parents constantly. Overall from my experience at The Gateway school it was a poorly run school that barely taught or prepared me or my peers for high school and beyond. Ps. if my grammar or spelling is bad that's just another testament to how little kids with get out of this school.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"James Minhwan Yoon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUtL_wp5gS3EWUJG598WSCuQRZ3NaLXRe8xXYX89OqDSwWZOn1PLw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kimberly Ehrlich","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXRi-m3-WDDbO2S30ZNRcOflZu47x-Bxq4twvCrpLW5-mvdSoLJ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"lex","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWNmDzT_Ucb-jaRvCS_L072l9ytIwXSHwGhqHcJ5aoqrYU12AIm=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"John Chen","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXeGlIMm4w_jnM-LfHcrg3LjWRmqGdRMKguuR6tBZGFudfXU_GI=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""}]

Like part of heart.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"MIKE WAI CHONG CHING","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWzqr2rTEwffUKldnBMscFaoOqEUS2EwvprQrP6U5h6EtLPKo3N=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Pro:)
- It keeps getting better and better
They offer SAT prep
They are organizing more and more outings
- Broadway shows
-Liberty Island Trip
-Community services
They provide students tutoring of all subjects
-especially English, they provide you all sorts of help to improve your pronunciation, writing, grammar, and public speaking skills.
- Very friendly and diverse community
-Multicultural festival, talent show are highlights of the school

Things that they need to kick it up a notch;/
- Home works are challenging but useful for college later
- There are rarely any club activities in the school
- More staff needed for counseling seniors that are graduating
- Limited space and facilities like science lab

My advice as an Alumni
- Immerse into the environment and improve English
- try to learn some basics about another language from others while you are in the school
- Talk with teachers, they are your best partners to improve your English and your best tour and city guide.
- Great soccer and basketball teams

The school is a good place because it is a collective effort, which means everyone plays their part even though it is insignificant. Over the past 2 years, I have noticed the improved qualities and services provided by the school but still, they couldn't fully satisfy your high school life if you don't take the lead.

BE the one who helps set up a club, be the one who encourages others to learn, be the one who says something good. It's not always about being the first one but it's about the mindset.
